change_comments.message:
Hi Shivian I think the code does what it intend but there are some (complicated) issues with naming. There is also some history related to the term altenateId/fdn you of course are not aware off (we are very strict on good consistent naming in CPS!). I'll try to organise a call with you later. I do have my doubt myself id in this case we CAN use the term FDN as you a specifically are converting jexPath to a FDN... Which happen to be what ericsson uses as AlternatIds (therin lies the problem) But you definitely use the term FDN to early in your current patch...
Might need a secodn opion (other team member) to dicuss some of this
but in teh mean time see hwo you get on with mu suggestions
Looking good, a few minor comments
great progress, just a little bit of tidying

inline_comments.message:
maybe rephrase and can say something like it can be a single or multiline

also suggestion maybe not using 'dataNodeSelector' and possibly sticking to JEX terms (https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_ts/132100_132199/132161/18.00.00_60/ts_132161v180000p.pdf) in this case dataNodeSelector = JexBasicExpressionWithManyPaths

e.g
Resolves alternate IDs from a JEX basic expression with many paths
a valid jex basic expression as path
jex basic expression with many paths
too long , not necessary for this specific test
seems repeated on other tests , probably covered already?
becomes hard to maintain and unreadable, if possible split to make it easier to read too :)

Can all the streams be chained? Something like:

return Arrays.stream(input.split("\\R"))
        .map(String::trim)
        .filter(line -> !line.isEmpty())
        .filter(line -> !line.startsWith("&&"))
        .map(JexResolver::resolveFdnFromSinglePath)
        .flatMap(Optional::stream)
        .map(JexResolver::toAlternateId)
        .distinct()
        .collect(Collectors.toList());
Just a checkstyle issue. Needs an extra indent
This is compiling the regex every iteration. Can it go out of the loop

In Patchset 1 I already wrote what you described here but as per the suggestion of @lee.anjella.macabuhay@est.tech I changed it to current version. Hi @gerard.nugent@est.tech and @lee.anjella.macabuhay@est.tech  can we come to a conclusion here on the recommended way?
Sorry, I didn't spot that. It was just a suggestion. Feel free to stick to Lee Anjellas suggextion

jexBasicExpressionWithManyPaths 

there are some qualifiers in the name that dont make sense unless you would have different methods for different variations of Jex
'basic' its not like we complex version  too?
'many' well you dont know until you have examined it. From the code it could even be empty. I see in teh private method use use 'single' which is fine because you have check/manipulated and only use that method for those variations (I assume)

Finally Jex stands for JsonExpresion so JexExpression is liek a half expanded acronym :-( Although I see it being used in website too)

so all combined

I recommend just 'JsonExpression' (preferred) or 'jex' for this parameter
why change from 'Paths' to 'Lines' I think these are still path ie trimmedPaths
dont use the term FDN use alternatId everywhere instead

distinctAlternateIds

But I probably would combine this line with the return statement and then you dont need this variable name
is this really possible. ie double slashes?
even better shoudl be dined as a meaningfully named (private) constant. There are many instance of such already oin our code
extract out private constant with meaningful name please
extract out private constant with meaningful name please

took me a minute but this is regex for '=' + group 1 correct?
So again extracting out  this in a constant would be good for readability


alternateIdIdentiferAssigmnetUsingRegexGroupOne (it is hard to come up with a good name)
we dont put javadocs on the private methods.
no javadoc needed on private methods.

code formatting is incorrect.
check if you have the correct formatter setup.
Nver use .* import. Setup your IDE to prevent this
i suspect this fits on 1 line (preferred)
note sure if this class should be static, interested  to get others opinion too...
make sure you have  unit test for this (I didn't sugget it in my email)

suggestion: extract out as method so we can give it a meaningful name

splitIntoLocationPathsSegments(locationPath)
Build will fail on this in Gerrit also

public methods should come before private methods
(your build should fail -checkstyle- because of this)

FYI, we never comment on it but officially the convention for the title (def' of a test is that it should start with a capital and end with a full stop.
So in this case and all tests below you are just missing the full stop.

NB: i would prefer you us ids (lowercase) in descriptions, javadoc etc.
(I think that is what we do most time but probably not 100% consistent)
remove the word 'only' (nothing is being ignored here)
only one unique path with id is returned

not needed
TIP: whenever a column in a test table has the same value for all rows it should NOT be in the table.
In this case it is also better (more readable) to simplify with using isEmpty, see previous comment
you mean method not the class right?
NB: dint take my comment too literal (i might not have selected exact ection or made typos myself), this should have been removed too :-D
is it required to wrap in another list??
I worked on that with Shivani and we had to do this but maybe once we use the list interface it will be fine (should have spotted that myself)
still needed ? dont think so , also based on prev comments
Arrays.asList returns an unmodifiable list hence it is need to be wrapped in another list

yeah. its needed to make it mutable.
Arrays.asList returns a fixed size list which will only allow replace.
we cant add or remove an item from that.

Wrapping needed to make it mutable.

the above can be done using Streams as well.

return Arrays.stream(locationPath.split(SEGMENT_SEPARATOR))
        .skip(1) // skip root
        .collect(Collectors.toList());
